stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the highway route) yields a guaranteed return of $93K. Option B (the scenic route) depends on traffic conditions: with probability 33-53% (uncertain), it yields $202K; otherwise it yields $29K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in traffic conditions affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $93K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $86.1K to $120.7K. Point estimate: $103.4K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 37.0%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 37.0%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
